codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully creates comprehensive E2E Playwright tests covering all four cross-area flows specified in the feature description. The tests verify VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-004, plus validate VAL-DASH-001 and VAL-DASH-002 (dashboard loading and theme persistence). The code is well-structured, properly documented, and includes robust helper functions for user creation and lifecycle management.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The existing shared auth helpers (tests/e2e/helpers/auth.ts) use getByLabel('Work email') but the actual LoginPage uses , causing a mismatch. The worker correctly identified this pre-existing issue and created custom login helpers that use 'Email' instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff.discoveredIssues[0]: 'The shared auth helpers use getByLabel(Work email) but the actual LoginPage uses Email. The existing auth-navigation E2E tests would fail with the current login page code.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: DropdownMenu items in the user actions dropdown are not visible in the Playwright accessibility tree. Worker had to use JS evaluation workarounds. This suggests either the component needs data-testid attributes or Radix-compatible selectors.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff.discoveredIssues[1]: 'DropdownMenu items in the user actions dropdown are not visible in the Playwright accessibility tree. They need to be clicked via JS eval rather than standard Playwright selectors.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill's TDD procedure was appropriately deviated from for this verification-focused feature. The worker correctly noted that E2E verification features benefit from browser-first verification before writing test code.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[0]: 'Verified all flows via agent-browser first, then wrote Playwright E2E tests. This is a verification-focused feature, not an implementation feature.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker appropriately did not invoke frontend-skill since this feature was purely about writing E2E tests with no UI implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[1]: 'Did not invoke frontend-skill. This feature is purely verification/testing — no UI implementation or changes.'

summary: The cross-area-e2e-flows feature is fully implemented and verified. The worker created a comprehensive Playwright test suite (tests/e2e/cross-area-flows.spec.ts) with 421 lines covering: (1) VAL-CROSS-001: Full admin workflow from login through sign out with user creation; (2) VAL-CROSS-002: Theme persistence across navigation and page reload; (3) VAL-CROSS-003: Mobile full flow at 375px with drawer navigation and card layouts; (4) VAL-CROSS-004: Complete user lifecycle (create→edit→suspend→unsuspend→force reset→delete). All tests pass against the real backend. The worker also identified two pre-existing issues with the auth helpers and dropdown accessibility that should be addressed in future work but do not block this feature.
